Ankush Das
2h
Sarvam recently open sourced its models, but what else? 🧐 The open-source community isn't simply accessible with just the models available out there on Hugging Face. There's more to it, and I think that might matter more to build its early traction for the developer ecosystem. It could be a thing that can be solved quickly — but definitely something that could affect the pace of adoption by developers. I speak to a couple of experts including Shayak Mazumder (CEO, Adya.ai ) and Sudipta Biswas (Cofounder at Floworks ) to get an opinion on it and to simply highlight what it could mean — and what could be the next steps for players like Sarvam to do better right after open sourcing the models. https://lnkd.in/g-CE5k3J

Great session at the Adya Launch Lab Roadshow today at KIT – Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology. It was inspiring to see how Adya is building an ecosystem where students and founders can access the tools, mentorship, and resources needed to turn ideas into real startups—all in one place. Learning about the startup journey, AI innovation, and how technology can be translated into impactful solutions was truly valuable. A special thanks to CEO Shayak Mazumder for sharing valuable life lessons and insights from his entrepreneurial journey. Also thanks to the engineering team Sowraj M and Hemanth S for guiding us through the working of the platform and giving us a deeper understanding of how it supports builders and innovators. Grateful as well to the Dean of Computing, Vimal Shanmuganathan , at KIT-Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology for the continued support and encouragement towards student innovation and technology-driven learning. This experience was a great opportunity to learn more about the startup ecosystem and connect with people who are passionate about building innovative solutions. Looking forward to exploring more in AI/ML, building meaningful projects, and contributing to the growing AI community. hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AIInnovation hashtag # StartupEcosystem hashtag # AI hashtag # StudentBuilders

A
Adya
2d
A powerful step forward for the AI builder ecosystem in India. We’re proud to see the Adya Launch Lab inaugurated at KIT-Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology Institute of Technology, Coimbatore, marking the beginning of a deeper collaboration between academia and industry to shape the next generation of AI innovators. The inauguration, led by Adya Founder & CEO Shayak Mazumder , represents more than just a lab launch, it represents a shared commitment to giving students access to the tools, platforms, and mentorship needed to build real-world AI systems. The Adya Launch Lab at KIT will serve as a hub where students can: • Explore Agentic AI and Generative AI technologies • Build real-world AI products and intelligent systems • Work with modern AI platforms and development tools • Engage with industry-driven research and startup innovation Our goal with Launch Lab is simple: Enable students to move from learning AI to building AI. We are grateful to the leadership and faculty at KIT-Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology Institute of Technology for their vision in fostering an ecosystem where technology, research, and entrepreneurship come together. A special thank you to the entire KIT community for the incredible enthusiasm and support in making this initiative possible. This is just the beginning. The future of AI will be built not only in labs and companies, but also in campuses where the next generation of builders are learning, experimenting, and creating. hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AgenticAI hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # AIInnovation hashtag # IndustryAcademia hashtag # FutureOfAI hashtag # TechEducation hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # InnovationIndia

D
Dharshika D.S
2d
🌟 Excited to share my experience at the Adya Launch Lab at KIT - Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology! The session led by Shayak Mazumder , CEO & CTO of Adya.ai , along with the brilliant team, offered incredible insights into how platforms like Adya are empowering students and innovators to transform ideas into real-world AI solutions. Their demonstration highlighted how AI tools, guidance, and resources can support every stage of innovation—from ideation to execution. I would also like to extend my gratitude to Dean Vimal Shanmuganathan , whose encouragement inspires students to explore emerging technologies and take on challenging projects. 🙏 💡 Building on this inspiration, I created an Agentic AI project: “AutoML Agent: Autonomous Data Scientist” 🤖 This agent automates the entire data science workflow: 📥 Input: User-uploaded dataset + query (e.g., “Analyze this dataset and suggest the best ML model”) 🛠 Workflow: 1. Dataset Analyzer Agent – Understands dataset structure, detects missing values, and identifies target variables 2. Data Cleaning Agent – Handles missing data, encodes categorical variables, and normalizes features 3. Model Selection Agent – Automatically selects suitable ML algorithms like Random Forest, Logistic Regression, or Gradient Boosting 4. Model Training & Evaluation Agent – Trains models and evaluates them with metrics like Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1 Score, and Confusion Matrix 5. Insight Generator Agent – Provides actionable insights and recommendations This project gave me hands-on experience with autonomous AI agents, demonstrating how AI can simplify complex data science workflows and generate meaningful insights efficiently. 🚀 hashtag hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab

S
Shayak Mazumder
2d
🚀 Adya is Hiring — Founders’ Office Intern We’re looking for a sharp, highly reliable intern to work directly with the founders at Adya . You’ll help run AI-driven projects, research initiatives, reports, and internal coordination using modern AI and agentic tools. This role involves: * Working with business leaders and internal teams * Gathering information and creating structured reports * Using AI tools to automate and execute tasks * Doing independent research and solving problems We’re looking for someone who is: * Extremely diligent and dependable * Very comfortable using AI tools * Able to work independently with minimal handholding * Curious and proactive This is a rare opportunity to see how AI systems and companies are built from the inside. If interested, DM your CV or share this with someone who should apply. Please include a cover letter mentioning any relevant experience such as placement committee member using AI to optimise tasks, previous founders' office experience, organising clubs and events using AI to optimise your work. Include the projects you have executed to prove your previous experience. Only candidates who can prove similar work experience will be considered.

P
Praveen R
4d
Great Learning Experience at Adya.ai Launch Lab Today I had the opportunity to attend the Adya.ai Launch Lab Programme, and it was an incredibly insightful session. The discussion helped me understand more about Artificial Intelligence, AI platforms, and how developers can build intelligent automation systems using modern AI tools. During the session, I explored the Adya.ai platform, which allows developers to create AI-powered workflows and applications efficiently. As part of my learning, I also built a small AI-powered News Assistant project. In this project: • I used an HTTP API request to fetch real-time news data. • The data is processed and sent to Gemini AI, where the AI reads the structured JSON data. • Gemini then analyzes and summarizes the news, converting raw API data into meaningful insights for users. This project helped me understand how API integrations, AI models, and automation workflows can work together to create intelligent applications. Sessions like this truly inspire me to explore more in the field of AI, automation, and real-world AI product development. A special thanks to Shayak Mazumder – CEO & CTO, Adya.ai for sharing valuable insights and encouraging students to innovate with AI. Looking forward to building more AI-driven solutions and learning continuously in this exciting field! hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # AI hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # LaunchLab hashtag # AIProjects hashtag # StudentDeveloper hashtag # Innovation hashtag # TechLearning hashtag # FutureOfAI

A special thanks to Vimal Shanmuganathan , Dean (SoC & ET), for his strong support in enabling this initiative and bringing together such an enthusiastic community of builders at KIT. And to the ecosystem partners helping power the Adya Launch Lab movement across India: IDFC FIRST Bank Startup India Headstart Network Foundation IBM Ventures This is what Adya Launch Lab is about. I want to thank Vimal Shanmuganathan and the KIT-Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology for their hospitality and enthusiasm. I was very impressed with the level of commitment from the college and staff towards providing the students with the highest degree of training and opportunities. I reviewed ten student projects and was pleasantly surprised with their depth of product thinking. Several students connected with him separately to seek guidance on their projects. I strongly believe that the future of India and AI lies with the next generation. We need to empower them and provide them with opportunities to become job providers instead of job seekers. I also want to thank IDFC FIRST Bank for their support in all the hashtag # Launchlab programs across India. 🙏 Adya

Today, I had the opportunity to attend an insightful session conducted by Adya at KIT-Kalaignarkarunanidhi Institute of Technology , where we explored how modern AI orchestration platforms are transforming the way applications are built and deployed. What made the experience even more exciting was the hands-on session, where we created a sample AI workflow and understood how AI agents can automate complex processes with minimal effort. It was fascinating to see how the future of development is shifting towards AI-driven systems, rapid deployment, and intelligent automation. A big thank you to Shayak Mazumder , CEO of Adya , and the entire team for conducting such an engaging and insightful session. A
Arun Pandit ®
1w
Some people leave a strong impression the very first time you interact with them. I first met Shayak Mazumder sir virtually a few years ago during a panel discussion at the E-Log Asia Summit, hosted by Max Henry and the Global Supply Chain Council (GSCC) . At that time he was the CEO & Co-Founder of Eunimart , and what stood out immediately was his clarity of thought and structured approach to problem solving. We later met in person while I was representing Hyphen SCS at the TiE Global Summit (TGS) hosted by TiE Hyderabad , and most recently again at the India AI Impact Summit 2026 . Very few people combine clarity, conviction and execution the way Shayak Mazumder sir does. Today, as the CEO of Adya , he is building what I personally consider one of the most advanced yet practical AI platforms emerging from India. Through platforms like ATMA and Vanij, Adya enables organizations to build and deploy AI-driven applications across domains such as retail, finance, insurance, education, and jobs — deployable across multiple clouds (AWS, Azure, GCP) and digital commerce networks like ONDC. What impressed me most is the balance between sophistication and usability. Adya’s ADI (Adya Deep Intelligence) models are often 20–40x more economical than traditional LLM deployments, while supporting powerful multi-agent workflows for cataloguing, search, inventory management, logistics, pricing, customer support and more. Platforms like these can dramatically lower the barriers to AI adoption. It is not just about large enterprises anymore. Solutions like Adya can empower SMEs, startups, and even universities, making AI affordable, easy to deploy, and usable for the long tail of businesses and institutions. During the summit, Shayak Mazumder sir also gave a live demonstration of the platform to students from my alma mater Sainik School Sujanpur Tira , while guiding them through the First Principles approach to problem solving — encouraging them to break problems down to their fundamentals and rebuild solutions from the ground up. Personally, interacting with him always carries an added sense of respect. His Indian Navy background as a Lieutenant Commander and my Sainik School roots, create an instant bond built on discipline, service, and purpose. Encouraging to see advanced yet easy-to-implement AI platforms coming out of India — with the power to democratize AI for the long tail. hashtag # ShayakMazumder hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # ArunPandit hashtag # HyphenSCS hashtag # MaxHenry hashtag # GSCC hashtag # AIImpactSummit hashtag # AgenticAI hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # BuildInIndia hashtag # ONDC

S
Sainik School Sujanpur Tira
1w
Learning becomes powerful when students get to see ideas in action. At the India AI Impact Summit 2026 , the cadets of Sainik School Sujanpur Tira had the privilege of interacting with Shayak Mazumder sir, CEO of Adya — an AI pioneer working at the forefront of Agentic AI, Generative AI, and enterprise-scale artificial intelligence systems. What made the interaction truly special was that he didn’t just speak about AI — he demonstrated it. He gave the cadets a live demonstration of Adya’s AI platform, showing how advanced multi-agent systems can autonomously perform complex enterprise tasks, orchestrate workflows, and dramatically simplify how businesses deploy artificial intelligence. For many of the students, this was their first real exposure to enterprise-grade AI systems in action, and it opened their minds to the possibilities of what they could build in the future. Equally impactful was the way he guided the students to think. Shayak sir introduced them to the First Principles approach — encouraging the cadets to break down problems to their most fundamental truths and rebuild solutions from the ground up, rather than relying on assumptions or conventional thinking. Drawing from his diverse journey — from serving nearly a decade in the Indian Navy as a Lieutenant Commander to building deep-tech AI platforms used by enterprises — he emphasized the importance of discipline, clarity of thought, and structured problem-solving in both leadership and innovation. The cadets were deeply inspired by the clarity, passion, and rigour with which he shared his experience and knowledge. We also extend our sincere gratitude to our alumnus Arun Pandit ® , Co-Founder of Hyphen SCS , for facilitating this invaluable interaction and consistently helping bridge the gap between students and global industry leaders. The cadets represented the school with distinction at the India AI Tinkerpreneur 2025 Bootcamp under the Atal Innovation Mission, where they were selected among the Top 50 Projects. 🔹 Team 1: Virtual Tech Guard Innovators: Cdt. Shaurya Malik & Cdt. Akhilesh Singh An AI-driven security and monitoring solution designed to enhance safety through intelligent monitoring and detection. 🔹 Team 2: Fabricate Me AI (Smart Virtual Fashion Try-On) Innovators: Cdt. Arnav Vashist & Cdt. Aditya Negi An AI-powered virtual fashion try-on platform aimed at transforming the digital retail experience through smart visualization and personalization. At SSST, we believe exposure to visionary leaders and real-world technology is essential to shaping the innovators and leaders of tomorrow. We are grateful to Shayak Mazumder , sir, for inspiring our cadets and to Arun Pandit ® for continuing to strengthen the bond between alumni and institution. Moments like these remind us that innovation grows fastest when knowledge is shared generously. hashtag # SainikSchoolSujanpurTira hashtag # AIImpactSummit hashtag # AtalInnovationMission hashtag # YoungInnovators hashtag # AILeadership hashtag # shayakmazumder hashtag # arunpandit

Prathamesh Ambokar
1w
2025 was the year I chose courage over comfort, the year I stopped shrinking myself to fit familiar spaces and started stepping into rooms that once intimidated me. Pixels Creative Technologies launched its first project out of Australia Stemware Studio — a quiet reminder that dreams grow when you dare to move. We unveiled for our first AI client Shayak Mazumder Archana . at the NVIDIA Summit, and in that moment, the weight of every late night turned into pride. It wasn’t just a launch, it was proof that we belonged. Meeting Sajith Ansar and witnessing his journey of building Unlimits showed me what conviction looks like in motion. At @GHCI, surrounded by relentless ambition and voices like Bhumi Pednekar, I saw the future of women led tech being bold, diverse, and unapologetic. Experienced transformative innovation and collaboration with world-class leaders at the IndiaAI Summit Sundar Pichai Jensen Huang Piyush Goyal Marc Benioff Dario Amodei . The National Institute ofDesign (NID) Ahmedabad Alumni Meet brought me back to my roots. The round table at ATLAS SkillTech University and countless founder conversations reminded me that growth doesn’t happen in isolation - it happens when you show up, speak up, and lean in. 2025 wasn’t just about networking. It was about rewriting the story I told myself about what was possible. If you’re waiting for a sign to step forward, this is it. The rooms you’re afraid to enter might just be the ones that change your life.

Shayak Mazumder
3w
This was such an immense opportunity to meet Shradha Sharma , the founder of YourStory Media . Shraddha is an inspiration to all founders in India. She has been supporting start-ups with all her effort for the last two decades. I find people like Shraddha have been doing an unpaid service to start-ups like us. I absolutely loved our interaction. I got a chance to position Adya . One of the key comments that I made was that 99% of the solutions I've seen at the India AI Impact Summit can be built in a few months by a few developers on the Adhya platform. It's a platform where anybody can build anything by asking the AI. The AI does the orchestration and the building for you. Imagine if there exists a platform from India that combines the best of Claude Code + N8N + Replit and put it into one, where you can also build your own models and LLMs, what would be the impact? That platform exists. It's called Adya . In the last 3 months, about 20,000 agent systems have been built on Adya. So cheers to YourStory Media , cheers to Adya , and cheers to innovation from India!

R
Rashi Gupta
3w
I've been listening to multiple sessions from the India AI Summit. And this line has been living in my head rent-free since: "AI karlega kaam to, but kya karwana hai becomes important." Shayak Mazumder said this. AI will do the work. But what work you get it to do — that's where the real thinking lives. He shared stories from across domains — defence, healthcare, drug discovery, and education. His point: everybody should be able to build what they want to build. The masses are becoming creators. But understanding doesn't come until you actually go out and do the work. That's what makes first principles thinking non-negotiable. Manik Pasricha shared similar thoughts. Companies like Emergent are generating $500K revenue per employee, 10x what traditional IT manages. You don't need a tech co-founder anymore. You can ship an MVP by thinking and prompting. So if everyone can build, what's the edge? Solving the customer problem backwards — starting with what customers actually need, not building forward an AI-native product hoping a problem shows up. India is sitting on first-degree problems. Healthcare, governance, civic tech, consumer tech. High-impact opportunities that don't need AI washing. They need someone who understands the problem deeply enough to know where technology actually fits. First principles thinking. Customer empathy. When everything else is replicable by AI, these become the differentiators. Also, a big shoutout to IndiaAI for putting these sessions online and making these conversations accessible to anyone willing to listen. hashtag # IndiaAISummit hashtag # AI hashtag # FirstPrinciples hashtag # BuildForIndia hashtag # ProductManagement

INSPIRE 2026 concluded with one clear message: Enterprise AI is no longer a pilot conversation - it is a leadership priority. Over the past two days in Udaipur, Adya had the opportunity to engage with the global leadership of Tech Mahindra Business Process Services at their flagship strategy forum. With our CEO Shayak Mazumder on the ground, the discussions moved beyond technology showcases to what truly matters for large-scale transformation: How multi-agent systems reshape enterprise workflows Governance-first AI for production environments Platform-led deployment across business units Measurable outcomes across BFSI, government, retail, energy, healthcare, and pharma At the Adya experience zone, we demonstrated our 5-level enterprise AI architecture - designed to help organizations move from fragmented AI initiatives to a unified, scalable, and secure implementation. What stood out most was the shift in the room: From “Where can we use AI?” to “How do we operationalize AI across the value chain?” We thank the Tech Mahindra BPS leadership and global teams for the high-impact conversations and the opportunity to be part of a forum that sets the strategic direction for the year ahead. Udaipur was about alignment. What follows is execution. Honoured to participate in the India AI Impact Summit 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, where I spoke in the STPI-led session on “Social Empowerment via AI Accessibility.” The discussion brought together diverse perspectives from technology, policy, and venture ecosystems, focusing on how AI can transcend efficiency to become a true equalizer for society. We explored practical pathways to enhance AI accessibility, inclusivity, and outcome-driven solutions for citizens, enterprises, and public systems. From a FarEye perspective, this conversation holds particular significance. As we implement AI at scale across logistics and delivery ecosystems, we witness firsthand how intelligent platforms can enhance reach, reliability, and real-world access, rather than merely optimizing operations. Responsible, scalable AI can reinforce essential networks that people rely on daily. It was a privilege to share the panel with distinguished leaders and exchange ideas on: • Making AI usable and reachable at scale • Responsible and inclusive AI deployment • Bridging ecosystem gaps between innovation and impact • Public–private collaboration for social good I am grateful to the organizers for curating a meaningful and action-oriented dialogue, and to the audience for their thoughtful engagement and questions. We Are Live At INDIA AI IMPACT SUMMIT 2026 - From Infrastructure to Intensity! hashtag # Day3 At the IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026, during the CII-led segment on AI, we set out to discuss the 5 Layers of AI — ⚡ Electricity 🏗 Data Centres & Infra 🧠 Compute 🤖 AI Models 📱 Applications Somewhere between compute and models… my fellow panelist Shayak Mazumder and I got so carried away that we forgot there was an audience. We were deep in debate. Full throttle. Almost in a 1:1 boardroom mode. Thankfully, our amazing moderator Gautam Badalia stepped in and brought us “back to the panel” 😄 But honestly, that’s what happens when the conversation is real. When India’s AI stack is no longer theory — it’s being built layer by layer. What made it special: * We didn’t just talk models. * We spoke about power grids and sovereign infrastructure. * About compute access and building at scale. * About applications that are inclusive for Bharat. * About creative-first thinking in an AI-first era. * About the unstoppable power of Indian youth. * And above all, about the mindset to collaborate to win. The intensity was a reflection of something bigger — India isn’t participating in AI. India is architecting it. Grateful to Confederation of Indian Industry Madhurima Maniyara for curating such a sharp, future-focused forum. And to my fellow panelists for pushing the thinking beyond slides and into substance. Reflecting on the CII panel at the India AI Impact Summit 2026, moderated by Gautam (GRAIT Cloud), the recurring theme was that AI is becoming the new operating system for brands. It’s not just a “tool” to be bolted on; it’s a transformative force that reshapes how brands create, distribute, personalise, and measure everything from end to end. A few key takeaways from the panel: 1) Creativity and AI are evolving from campaigns to a continuous system. Brands are no longer just “making ads”; they’re building always-on experiences where creativity can scale responsibly. 2) Offline and small models offer significant opportunities. Small language models (SLMs) that function without internet access can dramatically alter reach stories. 3) AI can finally connect with populations that traditional media couldn’t. With the right language, cost, and distribution strategies, rural and underserved audiences become accessible in ways that were previously impractical. 4) India-specific research can become a real advantage. One particularly intriguing discussion revolved around using AI to accelerate the understanding of Indian genetics, enabling therapies and drugs to be more effectively targeted for our demographic, rather than being copied from global averages. 5) Cost unpredictability is a major barrier to adoption. While the “pay-per-token” model is suitable for experimentation, it often feels like an open-ended meter for MSMEs. Small businesses don’t just need low costs; they need predictable costs. This predictability is what transforms curiosity into actual adoption. Niraj Ruparel (Wpp), Shayak Mazumder (Adya), Manish (Oracle), and Rakesh (JetSynthesys) provided grounded perspectives, ranging from enterprise readiness to real-world scale and user expectations. Overall, the takeaway is that if we want AI to truly spread across India, we should focus less on “access” and more on outcomes that are reliable, affordable, and predictable. I’d love to hear your thoughts on the biggest barrier to AI adoption for MSMEs right now: skills, trust, or cost predictability? hashtag # AI hashtag # IndiaAI hashtag # MSMEs hashtag # BrandTech hashtag # CreativeAI hashtag # SmallLanguageModels hashtag # Genomics hashtag # ResponsibleAI hashtag # Cloud Confederation of Indian Industry Madhurima Maniyara hashtag # indiaaiimpactsummit2026 hashtag # indiaAIMission hashtag # CII4AIAdoption hashtag # CII4ResponsibleAI hashtag # CII4AIImpact

V
Varad Chalmale
3w
My first post about the India AI Impact Summit got me thinking. If AI's future is being shaped in rooms like this, who's actually ready to be in those rooms? I've been diving deep into this over the past few weeks. And I'm noticing a pattern. The conversation always starts with: "We want to deploy AI agents." But within 5 minutes, it shifts to: "Who's going to be accountable when it makes a mistake?" "How do we know it's not hallucinating in our core workflows?" "What happens when regulators ask us to explain a decision?" Here's what I'm realizing: The companies winning at AI aren't the ones with the best demos. They're the ones who figured out governance before they scaled. They're asking: Who owns the agent's mistakes? How do we audit what it's learning? How do we ensure it doesn't become a compliance liability? This is where the real work is. Not in making AI smarter. But in making AI trustworthy enough to run your business. That's the gap between a prototype and production. And honestly? It's the gap most companies are still ignoring. At the India AI Impact Summit, I'm watching for one thing: Who's talking about infrastructure, governance, and reliability. Not just intelligence. Because intelligence without accountability is just expensive guesswork. If you're at the summit and thinking about these questions too, let's talk. Hall 5, Pod 5 - MIB Pavilion | Feb 16-20 | 10 AM-6 PM Adya Shayak Mazumder Amit Srivastava hashtag # IndiaAIImpactSummit2026 hashtag # EnterpriseAI hashtag # AIGovernance hashtag # AIInProduction hashtag # TechLeadership

Varad Chalmale

The Mahakumbh of AI is happening. And it's in India. On a random day over a "chai" conversation: My cousin asked me, 'Who owns AI?'. I couldn't answer. Not because I didn't know the technical answer. But because the real answer is bigger. AI isn't owned by one company. It's shaped by governments, researchers, policymakers, enterprises, and founders. The people who shape AI, shape the future. Here's why this moment matters. For the first time, the minds influencing the global AI narrative are converging in India. At the India AI Impact Summit 2026. If the Mahakumbh brings together millions seeking direction once in a lifetime, this summit brings together the minds directing where billions of lives are headed. This isn't just another conference. It's a signal that India isn't just consuming AI, we're helping define its direction. When global AI conversations move from closed boardrooms to Indian soil, that's geopolitical shift. That's economic power. If you care about AI, pay attention. Because it's not just about models anymore. It's about governance. Ownership. Power. Rooms like this determine who leads the next decade. My cousin asked, "Who owns AI?" Maybe the better question is: Who gets a seat at the table where AI's future is shaped? This summit is one of those tables. And it's in India. Happy to share that I emerged as the Winner of BUILD@IDS, conducted as an ancillary event at the 20th India Digital Summit (IDS 2026), organized by the Internet and Mobile Association of India (IAMAI). I worked on the IAMAI problem track, which involved designing and building a foundational technology platform integrating structured learning with high-integrity certification exams. The selection process began with online submissions, followed by a national shortlist of finalists invited to present at IDS in Bengaluru, where my solution was selected as the winning entry for the problem statement. Being part of the summit was an amazing learning experience. I got to connect with professionals across the industry and explore startups and AI-driven enterprise solutions, which really expanded my perspective on current software trends and products. I’m incredibly grateful for the opportunity to interact with Satyendra Verma , Shayak Mazumder , and Prasad Anumula PMP®, CISM(Q), LSSBB . Conversations with them and learning from their industry experience added significant value to this journey. Through this hackathon, I was also introduced to the Adya platform, and I’m excited to explore building and deploying solutions using it. A sincere thank you to IAMAI, the organizers, volunteers, and everyone involved in making this event such a smooth and impactful experience. Grateful to the GNITS-CSE Department for constantly encouraging participation in opportunities like this. Very thankful to my family as well, especially for the last-minute travel support and encouragement that made this experience possible. Looking forward to further developing this platform in collaboration with the Internet and Mobile Association of India !

MyyHashstash

Bangalore has a way of cutting through the noise. We were there last week for the India Digital Summit & IDA 2026 Conference, and what stood out wasn’t the announcements or the tech demos. It was the shift in tone. The conversations felt more honest than aspirational. Less "look what we built". More “here’s what actually worked, and what didn’t.” Across sessions, a few things came up repeatedly: AI is now part of the stack, not the story Interactive campaigns are being planned earlier, not added later Success is being measured less by engagement and more by what users actually do There was also a lot of honest discussion around what’s still not working, especially AI-led ideas that look great but don’t change behaviour. Came back with a clearer lens: Start with the user’s decision. Build the experience around that. Then use AI where it genuinely helps. In that context, it was great to see our client Bajaj Finserv take home 3 awards in different categories of work, a solid reminder that clear thinking and well-executed ideas still stand out in a crowded space. Great meeting and exchanging notes with so many sharp minds over the week, tagging a few people we crossed paths with : Shayak Mazumder , Bhanu Prasanna Raju M V , Raghav G. , Raghav Jaitly , Vedant Mate Akshay Jain Maanav Seth hashtag # IAMAI hashtag # IDA2026 hashtag # InteractiveMarketing hashtag # AIMarketing hashtag # MarketingStrategy hashtag # IndiaDigitalSummit

L
LINGESWARAN A
1mo
I had a valuable learning experience exploring the Adya platform and gaining hands-on exposure to its end-to-end AI ecosystem. The session provided a clear understanding of how Adya structures AI product development through its integrated studios: Agent Studio for designing and orchestrating intelligent agents and workflows App Studio for rapidly building full-stack web applications on top of AI agents Model Studio for adapting and fine-tuning models for specific use cases Cloud Studio for deployment, scaling, and production management As part of the hands-on exercise, I built FRIDAY, a simple AI-powered farmer assistance web application using App Studio. The application integrates weather insights, market price trends, crop-related information, and an AI assistant to demonstrate how AI logic can be quickly transformed into a usable web interface. Additionally, I developed a basic AI agent that takes email content as input, processes it using Gemini, and generates concise summaries along with suggested replies. This use case helped me understand how agent workflows can be applied to practical, real-world problems. Overall, the experience emphasized the importance of execution-focused learning—building functional systems, testing practical use cases, and delivering deployable solutions rather than limiting work to conceptual prototypes. Thankful for the guidance and learning support provided during the session. S
Shayak Mazumder
1mo
Who says it and what platform they say it from matters a lot more than what's being said most of the time. I'm rather excited that Anthropic is so successfully proving the futility of the platform vs product debate, the build vs buy debate in such a smashing way. I wrote a bunch of blogs on this and the timing couldn't be better! When around 26th December, we mentioned that the platforms will eliminate these debates and create scenarios where build vs buy becomes one and AI creating AI is becoming real, it was a tough pill to swallow. I've been asked a few dozen times since why do I think that developers, SaaS and software products are going to be replaced with realtime systems being created on platforms which will act as OS or fabric for enterprises. The blogs could be one way to answer them. The market reaction in the US from what Anthropic, Claude Code, Claude Cowork, Openclaw etc have created could be a better answer. Two questions remain * What's our answer? More vertical AI systems? * What will happen to our engineering talent? l am certain that it will take a few months for the investor thesis to evolve. However, policy needs to evolve faster to empower the talent.

The future of AI will not be built in isolation. It will be built in rooms where developers, platforms, and real-world use cases meet. That’s exactly what happened at Google Cloud Builder Day - Bengaluru 2026, a gathering focused on hands-on innovation, cloud-native architecture, and the next generation of AI builders. Because the next wave of AI won’t come from slides. It will come from people who are building. A
Ankush Das
1mo
🧠 The AI Shift #5 is live! We break down the post budget sentiments here. In this latest deep dive for Inc42 Media , I unpack how AI is being embedded across governance, public platforms, and core digital infrastructure, with signals that stretch decades, not quarters. For the government, AI is now a force multiplier. For investors, it’s a long-duration infrastructure bet. For startups, it’s a more complicated story. The budget pushes compute, data centres, and sovereign AI capacity hard. Tax holidays till 2047. Safe harbour provisions. Mission-mode execution. All signs of long-term intent. This edition dives into: - Why AI is being treated as public infrastructure, not a venture theme - What the data centre and compute push really signals to capital - Why startup upside may remain indirect, not policy-led - How investors are shifting focus from models to execution and domain depth You’ll find perspectives from: Founders, policymakers, and investors across India’s AI ecosystem, including a post budget comment from Abhishek Singh (CEO, IndiaAI Mission). And, Ankush Sabharwal ( CoRover.ai ), Raj K Gopalakrishnan (KOGO AI), Pranav Pai (3one4 Capital), and Shayak Mazumder ( Adya.ai ) Plus: + Top AI developments across the globe and India + A startup spotlight for Equip - Helping You Hire Faster + Prompt of the week by Arjun Nagulapally (AIONOS) 📒 + The buzz about Moltbook, social media for AI agents 🤖 hashtag # AI hashtag # IndiaAI hashtag # Startups hashtag # Policy hashtag # Investor hashtag # Infrastructure

Natarajan Ramamurthy , VP – Data Engineering at Target, participated in a panel discussion on “Platforms Accelerating AI Deployment in Large Enterprises & Government” at the hashtag # IndiaDigitalSummit , hosted by the Internet and Mobile Association of India ( IAMAI ) in Bengaluru on January 29-30. Natarajan shared perspectives on what it takes to drive hashtag # AIatScale , highlighting the importance of democratizing AI tools, establishing strong data governance, and ensuring interoperability as critical enablers for successful enterprise AI adoption. “We see hashtag # AI as a force enabler for our teams. We’ve built a platform based on the core principle of ‘democratisation’. When you can enlist your team, it builds great excitement, and every person wants to learn and do more,” he said. At Target, we continue to invest in AI platforms and capabilities that empower teams, accelerate innovation, and scale responsibly across the enterprise. . hashtag # DigitalTransformation hashtag # TargetTech hashtag # Innovation India Digital Summit Ashok Panda Shayak Mazumder Prashant D Gadekar Vishal Sukheja

AI creates real impact only when it becomes accessible. At the India AI Impact Summit 2026, our CEO Shayak Mazumder shared the stage with ecosystem leaders for a focused discussion on: Social Empowerment via AI Accessibility Alongside: 1. Tarun Adlakha - Founder & CEO, Ashwath Ventures 2. Jignesh Vasani - SVP, FarEye 3. Dr. Harshil Sharma - Director, Government Relations, Indus Action 4. Moderator: Kunal Upadhyay - Co-Founder, IIMA Ventures & Translation Endeavors The conversation went beyond the usual AI narrative. It addressed the real questions: >>How do we make AI infrastructure usable across sectors? >>How do we unlock opportunity for talent beyond metro cities? >>How do we move from AI capability to AI inclusion? Because the next wave of AI leadership will be defined not just by innovation, but by access, deployment, and real-world empowerment. If you’re at the summit, continue this conversation with us: Hall 5, Pod 5 - MIB Pavilion, Bharat Mandapam 16–20 February 2026 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM Adya Amit Srivastava hashtag # IndiaAIImpactSummit hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # AIAccessibility hashtag # AIForImpact hashtag # IndiaAI hashtag # ResponsibleAI

𝐖𝐡𝐞𝐧 𝐔𝐧𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐁𝐮𝐝𝐠𝐞𝐭 2026 𝐰𝐚𝐬 𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐝, 𝐀𝐈 𝐬𝐡𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐝 𝐮𝐩 𝐜𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐥𝐲 𝐚𝐬 𝐩𝐮𝐛𝐥𝐢𝐜 𝐢𝐧𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞, 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐚𝐬 𝐚 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐮𝐩-𝐥𝐞𝐝 𝐝𝐢𝐬𝐫𝐮𝐩𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐬𝐭𝐨𝐫𝐲. 𝐒𝐨, 𝐰𝐡𝐚𝐭'𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡? To understand what that means on the ground, I spoke to founders and investors across the ecosystem. I've shared some insights below for a head start, you can find the complete rundown of my story here: https://lnkd.in/giZdWz_u Shayak Mazumder ( Adya.ai ) sees a mature, platform-first government approach, but points to missing clarity around procurement, compliance, and execution for startups. Ganesh Gopalan ( Gnani.ai ) and Ankush Sabharwal ( CoRover.ai ) believe the government is lowering infrastructure and compute costs first, which could still unlock application-led startup growth over time. From an investor lens, Brijesh Damodaran (Auxano Capital), Abhishek Prasad (Cornerstone Ventures) and Rahul Agarwalla (SenseAI Ventures) see AI platforms de-risking adoption but compressing margins, pushing startups toward defensibility through domain depth, proprietary workflows, and real-world distribution rather than models alone. hashtag # Inc42 hashtag # AI hashtag # UnionBudget2026

Balaji Selvaraj

Spent an inspiring day at Karpagam College of Engineering as part of the Adya LaunchLab Fellowship 2026 🌱 What impressed me most was how naturally the students gravitated toward building-first thinking. Instead of stopping at concepts, they jumped straight into designing, testing, and improving AI-driven solutions—questioning assumptions and experimenting with real system behavior along the way. The session turned into an active workspace rather than a workshop. Students explored how modern AI systems are put together, how models interact with data in live environments, and how agent-based approaches can be applied to solve practical problems. Watching them iterate in real time made the experience incredibly rewarding. Guidance from our CEO & CTO Shayak Mazumder of Adya added a strong systems perspective to the day, pushing conversations toward architecture trade-offs, deployment realities, and the challenges engineers face outside controlled demos. Working alongside the team and mentoring students as they shaped their ideas using Vanij was a highlight for me. It’s exciting to see such clarity and confidence emerging so early in their technical journey. Huge appreciation to Karpagam College of Engineering for the support and coordination. Eager to see how these ideas evolve as students continue building 🚀 hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # Vanij hashtag # AIFellowship2026 hashtag # AIBuilders hashtag # AgenticSystems hashtag # EngineeringMindset hashtag # StudentInnovation hashtag # Buildathon hashtag # FutureEngineers

P
Preethi Vigneswaran
1mo
I built an AI agent — and it changed how I think about business decisions. During Adya Launch Lab, I designed and built a Decision Debt Agent — an agent that identifies delayed or postponed business decisions and quantifies their hidden financial impact. The idea was simple, but powerful: What if every delayed decision came with a visible price tag? 🧠 What the Decision Debt Agent does: Detects decision delays (hiring, expansion, investments, migrations) Models the downstream impact on productivity and revenue Converts delay into measurable decision debt (₹ per year) Surfaces clear, actionable recommendations instead of vague warnings ⚙️ What I learned building this agent: AI systems aren’t just about responses — they’re about accountability Agents work best when they combine logic, structure, and context Real-world AI needs to be explainable, auditable, and decision-ready Using Adya’s agent-first platform, I moved beyond prompt-level experimentation and focused on: Designing agent workflows Structuring inputs and outputs Thinking in terms of production constraints and system behavior Grateful to Adya and Shayak Mazumder sir for creating a space where students learn to build AI systems, not just talk about them. Onward to building agents that make better decisions visible 🚀 hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # DecisionIntelligence hashtag # SystemsThinking hashtag # Buildathon

“99% of everything you see at this summit could be built by Adya in a single day.” Our CEO Shayak Mazumder said this to Shradha Sharma (Founder & CEO of YourStory Media ) India’s largest startup media platform, in a raw, unscripted conversation at the India AI Impact Summit. Not a pitch. A statement of capability. Here’s what the world needs to know about Adya’s AI moment: >>10,000 developers. 12,000 platforms. Built in 2 months. >>No LLMs. No GPUs. Small, efficient, sovereign AI. This isn’t a startup story. This is India’s AI story. India’s talent is not limited to a few pin codes. It lives in tier-2 cities. In tier-3 cities. In towns most maps don’t highlight. Adya is building the platform that brings them in. You don’t need funding. You don’t need a large team. You don’t need permission. You need the right infrastructure. And just like Shradha Sharma and YourStory gave India’s founders a global voice, this is the moment India’s AI builders get theirs. Watch the full conversation https://lnkd.in/dq_iCdvN If this moved you, come experience it live. We’re not just talking about the future of AI at the summit. We’re running it, in real time. Hall 5, Pod 5, MIB Pavilion, Bharat Mandapam 16–20 February 2026 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM Bring your hardest questions. We’ll answer them live. P
Preetha Murugesan
1mo
On 27.01.26, I attended an ADYA session that really resonated with how I’ve always liked to approach AI. I’ve consistently believed AI projects should go beyond technical success and actually help real people in meaningful ways. That session reinforced this mindset - AI isn’t just about what models can do, but about what people still want to understand. Doctors do explain medical reports, and they do it well. But people are naturally curious. After an appointment, many still wonder: What does this value really mean for my body? What could happen in the future if this continues? What small changes can I start making today? That’s where I saw space for AI to support, not replace, medical professionals. With this approach, I built ReportWise – an AI-powered Medical Report Explainer. The goal wasn’t just to read reports. It was to make them easier to live with and learn from. ReportWise: – extracts lab test data from uploaded PDFs and report images – identifies whether values are normal or need attention using calm visual indicators – explains each test in simple, everyday language – describes possible general effects if certain levels remain abnormal – suggests practical lifestyle and wellness habits people can follow – presents everything in a structured dashboard so users see insights, not just numbers This project reminded me that AI is powerful not just because it can analyze data, but because it can patiently answer the extra questions people carry after the doctor’s visit. The Adya session didn’t change what I believe about AI — it strengthened it. It pushed me to think more like a product builder and focus on clarity, usefulness, and trust. Still refining, still learning — but always building toward AI that helps people understand more, not feel more confused. Grateful to the Adya team, and Shayak Mazumder for encouraging builders to move beyond demos and focus on deployable, user-centric AI. hashtag # AI hashtag # Adya hashtag # HealthTech hashtag # AIProducts hashtag # BuildWithPurpose

A
Abi krisshnnan R.M
1mo
I recently worked on a full-stack notes sharing platform for students in Adya, designed to solve a persistent academic problem: fragmented study materials, lack of organization, and inefficient peer-to-peer sharing. 🔹 Student Notes Sharing Platform (EdTech Use Case) The platform enables students to upload, manage, and access study notes in a centralized system, organized by subject and contributor. I designed and implemented the system as a fully functional web application with both frontend and backend components. Key capabilities of the platform include: Secure note uploads with metadata (title, subject, contributor) Backend-powered storage and retrieval of notes Subject-wise browsing and filtering for faster access Download functionality for shared resources A structured upload workflow with validation and persistence From a system perspective, the project covers: Frontend UI built for clarity and usability Backend APIs to handle note management Database integration for structured storage Separation of concerns between presentation, logic, and data The platform reduces dependency on scattered messaging apps and cloud links, providing a single source of truth for academic notes. 💡 This project reflects my interest in building end-to-end systems, not just interfaces—focusing on real workflows, data flow, and scalability considerations in educational technology. I thank Adya for providing a wonderful application that saves a lot time hashtag # Adyalaunchlab hashtag # Buildathon Adya Shayak Mazumder SARAVANAMUTHU S

I recently had the opportunity to participate in an insightful session at my college focused on Adya, and it was a game-changer! The most fascinating part was diving into Multi-Agent Systems. Seeing how different AI agents collaborate to handle complex tasks—moving from a simple prompt to a full Project Requirements Document (PRD) and then to functional code—was amazing. Using the platform, I worked on "Smart Syllabus," an AI-powered educational platform designed to solve a major student pain point: academic overwhelm. Excited to share that I was selected as a Finalist at BUILD@IDS 2026 in Bengaluru for working on the Tata Steel problem statement focused on AI-led prevention of pilferage in rebar transportation. It was a great experience working on a real-world industry challenge and presenting alongside some incredibly sharp and like-minded people who are passionate about building practical AI systems. One key insight I’m taking away from the event came from Shayak Mazumder : while AI can now write code faster than many humans, the real focus ahead is on building strong foundations, designing intelligent systems, and orchestrating agentic workflows that solve real problems. That perspective strongly resonated with me as I continue working on applied AI systems. Grateful for the opportunity to present, learn, and interact with industry leaders. My experience building a Multi-Agent AI project with Adya AI I recently explored the Adya AI platform and built a project called RepairFlow AI+, focused on solving a real-world smart city problem. 🧩 About Adya AI Adya AI made it easy to design multi-agent AI workflows, where different AI agents handle different responsibilities like understanding user input, making decisions, and routing outputs. What I liked most is how the platform encourages clear, explainable decision logic, rather than black-box AI. 🏙️ About my project Using Adya AI, I built RepairFlow AI+, an AI-powered public complaint management system. In simple terms: Citizens describe a public issue in natural language AI analyzes the complaint The system decides how urgent the issue is It automatically routes the complaint to the correct municipal department Authorities can see transparent AI reasoning behind every decision 💡 What I learned How to design multi-agent systems How AI can be used as a decision-support tool in governance The importance of explainable AI in real-world applications How to structure AI workflows like production systems This was a valuable hands-on experience working with AI beyond basic chatbots. A heartfelt thank you to Shayak Mazumder sir for giving us the opportunity to explore and build on ADYA. Getting hands-on experience with such an advanced platform at this stage has greatly improved my confidence and understanding of applied AI. 🚀 Built a Privacy-First Student Mental Wellness Web Application with Adya Launch Lab I designed and developed a complete Student Mental Wellness Check-In application during my hands-on build experience with the Adya AI platform. The goal was simple but meaningful — create a safe, calming digital space where students can regularly track their emotions, reflect on their thoughts, and build healthier mental habits. Instead of just building another dashboard, I focused on crafting a beautiful, supportive, and interactive experience. 🌿 Key Features • Daily mood & stress check-ins • Emotion tracking with visual analytics (charts + trends) • Personal journal with local storage • Gamified “Wellness Garden” that grows with streaks 🌱 • Breathing exercises & self-care tips • Rule-based AI chat companion for gentle support • Fully frontend + privacy-first (no data leaves the device) 📚 My Learning Journey with Adya : Working on Adya AI was a great hands-on experience where I: • Rapidly prototyped full-stack-like apps . • Learned real-world debugging & build workflows • Optimized features to run efficiently with minimal cloud usage • Practiced transforming ideas → UI → working product • Explored deployment concepts using Google Cloud It felt less like a tutorial and more like building an actual product from scratch. Grateful for the mentorship, tools, and platform support that made this learning-by-building experience possible. A big thanks to Shayak Mazumder sir and the Adya team for providing mentorship,platform access and an industry-aligned environment to build this project. SAY YES TO NEW OPPORTUNITIES Here's a small anecdote which i would like to share with everyone... On December 15th, I walked into the Adya AI event at Chennai Institute of Technology as just another student in the crowd. I didn’t even fully know what to expect from the day. Then, the unexpected happened. The student ambassador team asked if I could host the event. With zero prior notice and exactly 15 minutes to prepare, I said "Yes." What followed was such a great experience: -> Coordinating the flow with the backend team and the CEO. ~> Hosting interactive sessions that bridged the gap between the platform and the audience. -> Discussing my own projects and passion for the AI/ML domain with the Adya team That one "YES" LITERALLY opened doors I didn't even know existed. I walked away not only with a deeper understanding of the Adya platform and a growing interest in AI/ML, but with a massive boost in my communication confidence. Huge thanks to Adya's CEO Shayak Mazumder sir and the rest of the Adya AI team - sushma billu mam, CCO Archana mam, Bhuvaneshwaran K sir and the rest of the team for being so welcoming and supportive and for the unexpected gift box! Special thanks to my college for providing the space for these "random" opportunities to happen. Also another special thanks to Dr. Prasina Sathish mam , Dinesh D. sir , Susan Mano Derry V mam for supporting me along the way... The takeaway? Sometimes the best opportunities don’t come with a formal invite. They come when you’re willing to jump into the deep end. hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # Leadership hashtag # PublicSpeaking hashtag # AIML hashtag # StudentGrowth hashtag # Networking

A thoughtful perspective from Shayak Mazumder, CEO of Adya.ai , on India’s evolving AI and startup landscape. The emphasis on data centers, treating startups as mature ecosystem participants, and de-risking across all layers of the AI stack is an important direction for long-term impact. Equally relevant is the identification of healthcare, BFSI, enterprise applications, and AI-led services as key focus areas for India’s next phase of growth. Well-articulated insights by Ankush Das and the Inc42 Media DataLabs team, offering a balanced view through both policy and startup lenses. Worth a read for anyone building, investing, or shaping AI in India.

This wasn’t just a buildathon — it fundamentally changed how I think about building AI systems. Through Adya launch lab, I got hands-on exposure to how modern AI products are designed beyond models — with a strong focus on systems thinking, pipelines, and real-world constraints that matter in production. The sessions went deep into: The evolution of LLMs and how to make practical fine-tuning vs. retrieval decisions Designing RAG pipelines and agent-based workflows that scale beyond demos Moving from prompt-level experimentation to deployable, auditable AI systems Using Adya ’s platform and Studio, I worked on a multi-agent AI system to address a real compliance challenge in fintech — where policy interpretation, logic generation, and monitoring were handled as coordinated agents, not isolated scripts. I’m also proud to be a Campus Ambassador for Adya Launch Lab, helping bring this builder-first, systems-thinking culture to campus — and encouraging students to move from learning → building → shipping. Excited to share my latest project: Smart Study Planner! 🎓📊 I built a Smart Study Planner using Adya AI, designed to help students organize their study time, track progress, and stay consistent with daily goals. ✨ Key highlights of the project: →Dynamic study schedule generation based on available hours →Subject-wise task planning and progress tracking →Daily focus & upcoming tasks view →Visual progress indicators for better motivation →Clean, student-friendly UI This project was an amazing hands-on experience where I explored AI-driven planning, UI design, and real-world problem solving. Huge thanks to Adya AI for providing such a powerful platform to turn ideas into working solutions. S
Shayak Mazumder
1mo
Such an incredible session at the Karpagam College of Engineering I was impressed by the quality of the students, the projects they showcased included llm fine-tuning and multi agentic systems! What was really impressive were the questions during the session. I was asked questions on everything from security to governance, unified interface to healthcare models, Yann LeCun and the new architectures to limitations of LLMs. I've sometimes received such questions in my sessions with senior experts and CXOs of large companies. However, I didn't expect to have such conversations in a classroom. Tbh, it shows what can be achieved if the students are given the right guidance and the freedom of expression. So often we prevent students from asking questions or talking chances in our education system. Herein I wanted to express my gratitude to the HoD of the AI ML department at the college. She is an ex CTO of tech companies, who completed her PhD in AI and is a thought leader who is ensuring that students understand and think about the future. More power to such institutions!

Yesterday, I built an Inventory Management System using React, developed entirely on the Adya platform. This experience encouraged me to move beyond simply implementing features and focus on real-world usability, clean workflows, and clear data representation. Adya enabled a smooth transition from concept to implementation to live preview, eliminating typical setup and tooling overhead and allowing me to concentrate on execution and problem-solving. N
Nandanaraj R S
1mo
Yesterday’s session at Adya Launch Lab (Buildathon) was more than just an event. It was a space to explore, build, and rethink how ideas turn into real, working solutions. The hands-on exposure to the Adya AI ecosystem, combined with practical building, made the experience focused, engaging, and genuinely impactful. During the event, I worked on SereneMind, a student mental wellness check-in application designed to support emotional awareness in a simple, safe, and privacy-first way. The idea behind SereneMind is to help students reflect on how they are doing through gentle check-ins, mood tracking, and supportive insights without turning mental health into something clinical or overwhelming. The focus was on creating a system that feels human, encouraging, and actually usable in a student’s daily life. Building SereneMind pushed me to think beyond features and focus on flow, purpose, and execution. From designing meaningful AI-driven interactions to ensuring responsible data handling, every part of the project was shaped with real users in mind. It was not about experimenting for the sake of it, but about creating something that could realistically grow into a helpful platform. This journey was made possible with the support of Adya AI, using tools such as Agent Studio, App Studio, and integrations with Google Cloud and Gemini APIs. These tools helped transform ideas into working components and allowed me to focus more on problem-solving rather than setup or infrastructure. A big thanks to the Adya team and mentors for creating an environment that truly encourages building, learning, and iteration. This is just the beginning for SereneMind. I’m excited to continue improving it, making it more refined, more thoughtful, and more impactful. Looking forward to building further, learning more, and shipping better solutions ahead 🚀 Adya Shayak Mazumder Kamal Nath Balaji Selvaraj SARAVANAMUTHU S hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # Buildathon hashtag # SereneMind hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # GoogleCloud hashtag # AI hashtag # Adyalaunchlab hashtag # Buildathon

Mr. Saravanavel E

This wasn’t just a buildathon — it fundamentally changed how I think about building AI systems. Through Adya launch lab, I got hands-on exposure to how modern AI products are designed beyond models — with a strong focus on systems thinking, pipelines, and real-world constraints that matter in production. The sessions went deep into: The evolution of LLMs and how to make practical fine-tuning vs. retrieval decisions Designing RAG pipelines and agent-based workflows that scale beyond demos Moving from prompt-level experimentation to deployable, auditable AI systems Using Adya ’s platform and Studio, I worked on a multi-agent AI system to address a real compliance challenge in EdTech. With access to GCP credits, the buildathon pushed us to actually build: implement pipelines, test assumptions, iterate fast, and think in terms of product readiness, not just proofs of concept. Grateful to Adya and Mr. Shayak Mazumder sir, Mr. SARAVANAMUTHU S , Mr. Kamal Nath , Mr. Balaji Selvaraj for creating an ecosystem that empowers students to think like AI engineers, not just model users. S
SOWNDHAR RAJ S
1mo
Had a valuable learning experience exploring the Adya platform and gaining exposure to its end-to-end AI ecosystem. The session provided a clear understanding of how Adya approaches AI product development through its integrated studios: Agent Studio – for designing and orchestrating intelligent agents and workflows App Studio – for rapidly building complete web applications on top of AI agents without heavy frontend or integration overhead Model Studio – for adapting and fine-tuning models for specific business use cases Cloud Studio – for deployment, scaling, and production management As a beginner exploring the platform, I found the availability of pre-built agents and templates extremely helpful in understanding real-world AI workflows. Using App Studio, it was insightful to see how quickly AI logic can be transformed into a functional web application. During the hands-on session, I built a simple AI agent that takes an email as input, processes it using Gemini, and generates a concise summary along with suggested replies — a practical use case that highlighted the platform’s ability to move ideas swiftly from concept to implementation. Overall, the experience reinforced the importance of an execution-focused approach: building real systems, testing practical use cases, and delivering deployable, human-centric AI solutions rather than just prototypes. Grateful for the learning and clarity this session provided. Looking forward to iterating, improving, and building further. S
SREERAM GOPAL L
1mo
I recently had the opportunity to participate in an insightful session at my college focused on Adya, and it was a game-changer! The most fascinating part was diving into Multi-Agent Systems. Seeing how different AI agents collaborate to handle complex tasks—moving from a simple prompt to a full Project Requirements Document (PRD) and then to functional code—was amazing. Using the platform, I worked on "Smart Syllabus," an AI-powered educational platform designed to solve a major student pain point: academic overwhelm. The project focuses on: >Automated Organization: Students upload a syllabus (PDF/Image), and the AI parses key deadlines and topics automatically. >Smart Study Plans: It generates a structured weekly schedule and syncs it directly with Google Calendar. >Interactive Learning: The "Study Hub" creates AI-generated flashcards and a chat-style "Quiz Mode" for active recall. >Progress Tracking: A clean, "Calm Tech" dashboard to visualize course completion and reduce anxiety. What makes Adya stand out for me: =>Unified Workflow =>Multi-Agent Intelligence =>Speed to Solution A huge thank you to Shayak Mazumder and the Adya team for giving us access and sharing such a clear, powerful vision of the future of AI-driven development. hashtag # Adya hashtag # SmartSyllabus hashtag # AI hashtag # MultiAgentSystems hashtag # EdTech hashtag # GenerativeAI hashtag # FutureOfCoding hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence

Exploring the Adya ecosystem and building AI Memory Guardian (a memory reminder + forgetfulness prediction app) made one thing clear: 👉 AI is no longer about impressive demos. It’s about shipping usable systems. Key takeaways from this experience: Designing multi-agent intelligence (observation, prediction, and nudge agents) Transforming passive reminders into proactive, risk-based nudges Building systems that anticipate user needs before failures occur What truly stood out was the execution-first mindset: ✔️ Build fast without getting stuck in infrastructure ✔️ Think in products, not just prototypes ✔️ Launch → validate → iterate → scale ✔️ Focus on market-ready solutions, not assignment-ready demos The Launch Lab framework reinforced a powerful lesson: If you’re not deploying and testing real use cases, you’re just consuming. Grateful for the clarity, tooling, and hands-on exposure. Special thanks to Shayak Mazumder and the Adya team for enabling builders to move from idea → execution → impact. Now it’s time to iterate, refine, and ship 🚀 hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # AIProduct hashtag # ProductThinking hashtag # Innovation hashtag # MultiAgentSystems hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # TechWorkshop hashtag # ShayakMazumder hashtag # Adyalaunchlab hashtag # Buildathon @ Adya Shayak Mazumder @kamal @balaji @saravanamuthu

SELVAMATHAN S
1mo
This wasn’t just a buildathon — it fundamentally changed how I think about building AI systems. Through Adya launch lab, I got hands-on exposure to how modern AI products are designed beyond models — with a strong focus on systems thinking, pipelines, and real-world constraints that matter in production. The sessions went deep into: The evolution of LLMs and how to make practical fine-tuning vs. retrieval decisions Designing RAG pipelines and agent-based workflows that scale beyond demos Moving from prompt-level experimentation to deployable, auditable AI systems Using Adya ’s platform and Studio, I worked on a multi-agent AI system to address a real compliance challenge in fintech — where policy interpretation, logic generation, and monitoring were handled as coordinated agents, not isolated scripts. With access to GCP credits, the buildathon pushed us to actually build: implement pipelines, test assumptions, iterate fast, and think in terms of product readiness, not just proofs of concept. I’m also proud to be a Campus Ambassador for Adya Launch Lab, helping bring this builder-first, systems-thinking culture to campus — and encouraging students to move from learning → building → shipping. Grateful to Adya and Shayak Mazumder sir for creating an ecosystem that empowers students to think like AI engineers, not just model users. I am pleased to share a project developed using the Adya Labs platform, focused on improving agricultural decision-making through technology. The project is an Agriculture Cropping Support Application that enables farmers to interact with the system using a voice-to-text agent, reducing dependency on manual data entry and improving accessibility for users with limited technical proficiency. This project was implemented as part of hands-on learning using Adya Labs, emphasizing real-world problem solving, user-centric design, and applied development skills. K
KARTHIKEYAN .N
1mo
Today wasn’t just another technical session — it genuinely changed how I think about building AI products. While exploring the ADYA ecosystem and working on agentic AI systems, one idea kept coming back to me: AI isn’t about projects that run on one machine. It’s about building products that real people can actually trust and use. As part of this, I built a Terms & Conditions Risk Analyzer — an AI agent that doesn’t just summarize legal text, but helps users understand how risky an agreement really is. Building this pushed me beyond copy-paste prompts and into real problem-solving: identifying high-risk clauses like arbitration, unilateral termination, data usage, and liability limits separating clauses into positive, neutral, and concerning categories generating clear, risk-based recommendations instead of vague summaries presenting the analysis visually so users can make decisions, not guesses The biggest learning wasn’t technical — it was a mindset shift: build systems, not scripts think like a product builder, not a student launch, validate, iterate, and improve If an AI app isn’t helping users understand risk and make better choices, it’s probably just consuming compute. Now it’s time to refine, stress-test, and ship Grateful to the ADYA team for encouraging builders to move beyond demos and focus on deployable, user-centric AI. Adya Shayak Mazumder hashtag # AI hashtag # Adyalaunchlab hashtag # Buildathon hashtag # adya hashtag # ShayakMazumer hashtag # Kamal hashtag # Balaji hashtag # Saravanamuthu hashtag # TermsAndConditions hashtag # AIProducts

Some of the most important AI conversations don’t happen on stage. They happen around the table with builders. On March 17, Adya is hosting a closed-door Founder Roundtable at T-Hub, Hyderabad, bringing together a curated group of B2B founders exploring the future of enterprise AI. This interactive session will introduce the Adya AI Platform, designed to help companies rapidly build and deploy: • Enterprise AI applications • Custom LLMs and SLMs • Agentic AI systems • AI-powered workflows operating across enterprise environments Founders joining the session will get early access to the platform, a live product demonstration, and the opportunity to explore real enterprise use cases for their own companies. The session will include: • Platform introduction, architecture and core capabilities • Live enterprise AI build, applications developed in real time • Hands-on exploration, founders testing AI workflows for their businesses We’re keeping this session intentionally small and interactive, with 10–20 B2B founders participating in a feedback-driven discussion on the future of AI-native companies. 📍 Google for Startups Accelerator Workspace, T-Hub (Hyderabad) 📅 March 17, 2026 ⏰ 2:00 PM – 4:00 PM If you’re building in AI, SaaS, or enterprise technology, this is a chance to explore how AI-native platforms can power real enterprise systems. Apply to join: https://lnkd.in/dP2XPkaF Let’s build the next generation of AI-native companies. hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AgenticAI hashtag # EnterpriseAI hashtag # AIStartups hashtag # StartupIndia hashtag # AIPlatform hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # AIInnovation

S
Sainik School Sujanpur Tira
1w
Learning becomes powerful when students get to see ideas in action. At the India AI Impact Summit 2026 , the cadets of Sainik School Sujanpur Tira had the privilege of interacting with Shayak Mazumder sir, CEO of Adya — an AI pioneer working at the forefront of Agentic AI, Generative AI, and enterprise-scale artificial intelligence systems. What made the interaction truly special was that he didn’t just speak about AI — he demonstrated it. He gave the cadets a live demonstration of Adya’s AI platform, showing how advanced multi-agent systems can autonomously perform complex enterprise tasks, orchestrate workflows, and dramatically simplify how businesses deploy artificial intelligence. For many of the students, this was their first real exposure to enterprise-grade AI systems in action, and it opened their minds to the possibilities of what they could build in the future. Equally impactful was the way he guided the students to think. Shayak sir introduced them to the First Principles approach — encouraging the cadets to break down problems to their most fundamental truths and rebuild solutions from the ground up, rather than relying on assumptions or conventional thinking. Drawing from his diverse journey — from serving nearly a decade in the Indian Navy as a Lieutenant Commander to building deep-tech AI platforms used by enterprises — he emphasized the importance of discipline, clarity of thought, and structured problem-solving in both leadership and innovation. The cadets were deeply inspired by the clarity, passion, and rigour with which he shared his experience and knowledge. We also extend our sincere gratitude to our alumnus Arun Pandit ® , Co-Founder of Hyphen SCS , for facilitating this invaluable interaction and consistently helping bridge the gap between students and global industry leaders. The cadets represented the school with distinction at the India AI Tinkerpreneur 2025 Bootcamp under the Atal Innovation Mission, where they were selected among the Top 50 Projects. 🔹 Team 1: Virtual Tech Guard Innovators: Cdt. Shaurya Malik & Cdt. Akhilesh Singh An AI-driven security and monitoring solution designed to enhance safety through intelligent monitoring and detection. 🔹 Team 2: Fabricate Me AI (Smart Virtual Fashion Try-On) Innovators: Cdt. Arnav Vashist & Cdt. Aditya Negi An AI-powered virtual fashion try-on platform aimed at transforming the digital retail experience through smart visualization and personalization. At SSST, we believe exposure to visionary leaders and real-world technology is essential to shaping the innovators and leaders of tomorrow. We are grateful to Shayak Mazumder , sir, for inspiring our cadets and to Arun Pandit ® for continuing to strengthen the bond between alumni and institution. Moments like these remind us that innovation grows fastest when knowledge is shared generously. hashtag # SainikSchoolSujanpurTira hashtag # AIImpactSummit hashtag # AtalInnovationMission hashtag # YoungInnovators hashtag # AILeadership hashtag # shayakmazumder hashtag # arunpandit

S
SUKUMAR PARIDA
2w
Over the past few weeks, I immersed myself in building, stress-testing, and rethinking what it actually takes to deliver an AI product end-to-end. As part of my journey with Adya, I worked on creating an AI-driven Memory Guardian — a system built to move beyond reactive reminders and toward predictive, context-aware assistance. This wasn’t about impressive demos. It was about building something practical, reliable, and aligned with real user behavior. Key lessons from the process: • Systems architecture matters more than isolated models • Clear problem definition beats complex algorithms • Users behave unpredictably — design for that • Early validation saves months of rework • Shipping constraints force smarter engineering decisions What changed for me? AI is a tool. Product thinking is the differentiator. Grateful for the mentorship, feedback, and the push to focus on execution over endless experimentation. Next step: refine, iterate, and ship. APEX AdSprint — AI Fest 2026 | Powered by Simbound India’s First 24-Hour Agentic-AI Driven Digital Marketing Hackathon : A landmark initiative driven by visionary leadership and industry integration, APEX AdSprint stood as a defining moment in building India’s next generation of AI-native digital marketing leaders. Students from Leading Institutions of India like Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee , Indian Institute of Technology, Patna and more participated in this 24-Hour Hackathon, testing strategic knowledge, presentation skills and solving real-time problem statements from leading brands of India like Adobe , SAS , Millipixels Interactive , Federal Bank , State Bank of India , Verka , Vodafone Idea Limited , AristaVault and many many more under one roof. We are deeply grateful to our respected Mr. Jai Inder Singh Sandhu ,Managing Director, CHANDIGARH UNIVERSITY , whose forward-looking vision, whose unwavering commitment to innovation, and belief in industry-integrated education made this transformative platform possible. We were honoured by the presence of Dr. Raviraja N Seetharam, PhD, MBA , Vice Chancellor, Chandigarh University, whose leadership continues to strengthen academia-industry convergence at a national scale. Special appreciation to our Technology Partner, Mr. Shayak Mazumder , CEO & CTO, ADYA AI, for enabling the Agentic-AI ecosystem and reinforcing the future of AI-driven marketing. The platform was further strengthened by distinguished global industry leaders: • Varun Patil - Director, CESIM, who announced multiple startup seed funding opportunities, empowering student entrepreneurs. • Mr. Harjeet S. Gulati - Group Chair & CEO — Cerebrent | Millipixels | Clarient, who unveiled Global Internship Opportunities, unlocking international career pathways. This pioneering initiative was strategically led by: Prof. Sanjeev Jain , Senior Director, whose leadership continues to shape future-ready academic ecosystems, and Prof. Ashish Kumar Srivastava (Global Award Winning Educator) , Associate Director (New Programs) — who has been instrumental in bringing industry connects and sponsorships. A special note of appreciation to Ms. Priyanka Banyal and Dr. Sahil Kohli , the driving forces behind the entire journey from conception to execution, ensuring this vision translated into national-scale impact. Operational excellence was seamlessly executed under the leadership of Ms. Prerna Dhawan (HOD, Operations) , Dr. Vipin Sharma (HOD BBA Apex) and Dr Sharneet Singh Jagirdar, FRM , ensuring flawless coordination and delivery of this high-intensity, high-impact platform.
