Here are 6 Startup Funding & Accelerator Opportunities Ending 16–22 Feb 2026 for hashtag # startups , hashtag # founders , and hashtag # innovators looking for hashtag # funding , accelerators, hackathons, and government-backed AI pilots across deep-tech, fintech, hardware, AI, and global trade: 1. Alibaba.com Global Export Enablement Challenge Deadline: 16 Feb 2026 Industry partnership program by Alibaba.com to onboard and scale Indian MSMEs globally. Certified Channel Partner status + revenue share + global mentorship + co-branded credibility. Ideal for DPIIT-recognised startups in B2B e-commerce, ExportTech, logistics SaaS, cross-border fintech, and digital marketing. 2. Chiratae Ventures Sonic DeepTech Program Deadline: 16 Feb 2026 Seed investment initiative by Chiratae Ventures offering rapid capital deployment. $500K–$2M funding + 48-hour fast-track decision + demo day access. Best suited for DeepTech, ClimateTech, AI, Robotics, Biotech, and Energy startups raising seed capital. 3. iAccelerate 4.0 – Tamil Nadu Hardware Accelerator Tamil Nadu Technology Hub (iTNT) Deadline: 18 Feb 2026 12-week hybrid hardware accelerator supporting prototype-to-market journeys. ₹7.5–15 lakh design & prototyping vouchers + mentorship + manufacturing readiness support. Ideal for Tamil Nadu-based hardware startups (TRL 2–9). 4. PFRDA Hackathon 2026 Startup Incubation and Innovation Centre, IIT Kanpur (incubatoriitk) Deadline: 19 Feb 2026 National fintech innovation challenge by PFRDA- Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority and Development Authority. Cash prizes up to ₹10 lakh + mentorship from PFRDA & IIT Kanpur experts + national showcase. Open to students, startups, MSMEs, and fintech innovators. 5. Adya Launch Lab AI – ALL AI Deadline: 20 Feb 2026 Free AI builder & startup acceleration initiative with lifetime AI platform access. Cash prizes up to ₹5 lakh + cloud/model credits + VC exposure + marketplace showcase. Perfect for AI developers, students, and early-stage AI startups. 6. IndiaAI Innovation Challenge 2026 (Ministry of AYUSH) Deadline: 22 Feb 2026 National AI challenge supporting governance & public health deployment. ₹25 lakh funded pilot opportunity + potential ₹1 crore performance-linked government contract. Best for DPIIT-recognised startups with market-ready AI solutions. 💡 Pro Tip: Government and corporate-backed programs prioritize deployable solutions, regulatory readiness, and strong founding teams. Highlight pilots, paying customers, or validated use cases wherever possible. Explore and apply before deadlines close through Funding Board at https://lnkd.in/gurzpFRt Assam Startup Aniket Kamthe Balaganesh P, Ph.D., Dewansh Poddar Dr. Ravi K. L
LINGESWARAN A
1mo
I had a valuable learning experience exploring the Adya platform and gaining hands-on exposure to its end-to-end AI ecosystem. The session provided a clear understanding of how Adya structures AI product development through its integrated studios: Agent Studio for designing and orchestrating intelligent agents and workflows App Studio for rapidly building full-stack web applications on top of AI agents Model Studio for adapting and fine-tuning models for specific use cases Cloud Studio for deployment, scaling, and production management As part of the hands-on exercise, I built FRIDAY, a simple AI-powered farmer assistance web application using App Studio. The application integrates weather insights, market price trends, crop-related information, and an AI assistant to demonstrate how AI logic can be quickly transformed into a usable web interface. Additionally, I developed a basic AI agent that takes email content as input, processes it using Gemini, and generates concise summaries along with suggested replies. This use case helped me understand how agent workflows can be applied to practical, real-world problems. Overall, the experience emphasized the importance of execution-focused learning—building functional systems, testing practical use cases, and delivering deployable solutions rather than limiting work to conceptual prototypes. Thankful for the guidance and learning support provided during the session. 🚀 From AI-generated base to product-ready application As part of my continued work with Adya AI, I recently built a Buyer Application and refined it through hands-on code edits. What the app supports: 📦 Order history with detailed status 🚚 Real-time shipment tracking 🔔 Order status notifications ⚙️ Profile & notification settings Adya helped me quickly generate a clean, production-ready starting point. From there, I: Tweaked components and layouts Adjusted logic and user flows Refined the UI for better usability Made the app feel product-ready, not demo-ready This experience reinforced an important lesson for me: 👉 AI is best used as a force multiplier — not a replacement for thinking or coding. The real value lies in: Faster starting points Less time spent on boilerplate More focus on product decisions and refinement This build strongly connects with what I learned during the AI Buildathon: Execution + iteration > over-engineering. S
Srinath RK
1mo
My experience building a Multi-Agent AI project with Adya AI I recently explored the Adya AI platform and built a project called RepairFlow AI+, focused on solving a real-world smart city problem. 🧩 About Adya AI Adya AI made it easy to design multi-agent AI workflows, where different AI agents handle different responsibilities like understanding user input, making decisions, and routing outputs. What I liked most is how the platform encourages clear, explainable decision logic, rather than black-box AI. 🏙️ About my project Using Adya AI, I built RepairFlow AI+, an AI-powered public complaint management system. In simple terms: Citizens describe a public issue in natural language AI analyzes the complaint The system decides how urgent the issue is It automatically routes the complaint to the correct municipal department Authorities can see transparent AI reasoning behind every decision 💡 What I learned How to design multi-agent systems How AI can be used as a decision-support tool in governance The importance of explainable AI in real-world applications How to structure AI workflows like production systems This was a valuable hands-on experience working with AI beyond basic chatbots. A heartfelt thank you to Shayak Mazumder sir for giving us the opportunity to explore and build on ADYA. Getting hands-on experience with such an advanced platform at this stage has greatly improved my confidence and understanding of applied AI. 🚀 Built a Privacy-First Student Mental Wellness Web Application with Adya Launch Lab I designed and developed a complete Student Mental Wellness Check-In application during my hands-on build experience with the Adya AI platform. The goal was simple but meaningful — create a safe, calming digital space where students can regularly track their emotions, reflect on their thoughts, and build healthier mental habits. Instead of just building another dashboard, I focused on crafting a beautiful, supportive, and interactive experience. 🌿 Key Features • Daily mood & stress check-ins • Emotion tracking with visual analytics (charts + trends) • Personal journal with local storage • Gamified “Wellness Garden” that grows with streaks 🌱 • Breathing exercises & self-care tips • Rule-based AI chat companion for gentle support • Fully frontend + privacy-first (no data leaves the device) 📚 My Learning Journey with Adya : Working on Adya AI was a great hands-on experience where I: • Rapidly prototyped full-stack-like apps . • Learned real-world debugging & build workflows • Optimized features to run efficiently with minimal cloud usage • Practiced transforming ideas → UI → working product • Explored deployment concepts using Google Cloud It felt less like a tutorial and more like building an actual product from scratch. Grateful for the mentorship, tools, and platform support that made this learning-by-building experience possible. A big thanks to Shayak Mazumder sir and the Adya team for providing mentorship,platform access and an industry-aligned environment to build this project. Excited to keep creating meaningful AI-driven applications that solve real-world problems 🌿 hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AI hashtag # StudentWellness hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # LearningJourney hashtag # GCP

Gururaghavendra Padmanaban
1mo
SAY YES TO NEW OPPORTUNITIES Here's a small anecdote which i would like to share with everyone... On December 15th, I walked into the Adya AI event at Chennai Institute of Technology as just another student in the crowd. I didn’t even fully know what to expect from the day. Then, the unexpected happened. The student ambassador team asked if I could host the event. With zero prior notice and exactly 15 minutes to prepare, I said "Yes." What followed was such a great experience: -> Coordinating the flow with the backend team and the CEO. ~> Hosting interactive sessions that bridged the gap between the platform and the audience. -> Discussing my own projects and passion for the AI/ML domain with the Adya team That one "YES" LITERALLY opened doors I didn't even know existed. I walked away not only with a deeper understanding of the Adya platform and a growing interest in AI/ML, but with a massive boost in my communication confidence. Huge thanks to Adya's CEO Shayak Mazumder sir and the rest of the Adya AI team - sushma billu mam, CCO Archana mam, Bhuvaneshwaran K sir and the rest of the team for being so welcoming and supportive and for the unexpected gift box! Special thanks to my college for providing the space for these "random" opportunities to happen. Also another special thanks to Dr. Prasina Sathish mam , Dinesh D. sir , Susan Mano Derry V mam for supporting me along the way... The takeaway? Sometimes the best opportunities don’t come with a formal invite. They come when you’re willing to jump into the deep end. hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # Leadership hashtag # PublicSpeaking hashtag # AIML hashtag # StudentGrowth hashtag # Networking

Yesterday’s session at Adya Launch Lab (Buildathon) was more than just an event. It was a space to explore, build, and rethink how ideas turn into real, working solutions. The hands-on exposure to the Adya AI ecosystem, combined with practical building, made the experience focused, engaging, and genuinely impactful. During the event, I worked on SereneMind, a student mental wellness check-in application designed to support emotional awareness in a simple, safe, and privacy-first way. The idea behind SereneMind is to help students reflect on how they are doing through gentle check-ins, mood tracking, and supportive insights without turning mental health into something clinical or overwhelming. The focus was on creating a system that feels human, encouraging, and actually usable in a student’s daily life. Building SereneMind pushed me to think beyond features and focus on flow, purpose, and execution. From designing meaningful AI-driven interactions to ensuring responsible data handling, every part of the project was shaped with real users in mind. It was not about experimenting for the sake of it, but about creating something that could realistically grow into a helpful platform. This journey was made possible with the support of Adya AI, using tools such as Agent Studio, App Studio, and integrations with Google Cloud and Gemini APIs. These tools helped transform ideas into working components and allowed me to focus more on problem-solving rather than setup or infrastructure. A big thanks to the Adya team and mentors for creating an environment that truly encourages building, learning, and iteration. This is just the beginning for SereneMind. I’m excited to continue improving it, making it more refined, more thoughtful, and more impactful. Had a valuable learning experience exploring the Adya platform and gaining exposure to its end-to-end AI ecosystem. The session provided a clear understanding of how Adya approaches AI product development through its integrated studios: Agent Studio – for designing and orchestrating intelligent agents and workflows App Studio – for rapidly building complete web applications on top of AI agents without heavy frontend or integration overhead Model Studio – for adapting and fine-tuning models for specific business use cases Cloud Studio – for deployment, scaling, and production management As a beginner exploring the platform, I found the availability of pre-built agents and templates extremely helpful in understanding real-world AI workflows. Using App Studio, it was insightful to see how quickly AI logic can be transformed into a functional web application. During the hands-on session, I built a simple AI agent that takes an email as input, processes it using Gemini, and generates a concise summary along with suggested replies — a practical use case that highlighted the platform’s ability to move ideas swiftly from concept to implementation. Overall, the experience reinforced the importance of an execution-focused approach: building real systems, testing practical use cases, and delivering deployable, human-centric AI solutions rather than just prototypes. Grateful for the learning and clarity this session provided. Looking forward to iterating, improving, and building further. SREERAM GOPAL L
1mo
I recently had the opportunity to participate in an insightful session at my college focused on Adya, and it was a game-changer! The most fascinating part was diving into Multi-Agent Systems. Seeing how different AI agents collaborate to handle complex tasks—moving from a simple prompt to a full Project Requirements Document (PRD) and then to functional code—was amazing. Using the platform, I worked on "Smart Syllabus," an AI-powered educational platform designed to solve a major student pain point: academic overwhelm. The project focuses on: >Automated Organization: Students upload a syllabus (PDF/Image), and the AI parses key deadlines and topics automatically. >Smart Study Plans: It generates a structured weekly schedule and syncs it directly with Google Calendar. >Interactive Learning: The "Study Hub" creates AI-generated flashcards and a chat-style "Quiz Mode" for active recall. >Progress Tracking: A clean, "Calm Tech" dashboard to visualize course completion and reduce anxiety. What makes Adya stand out for me: =>Unified Workflow =>Multi-Agent Intelligence =>Speed to Solution A huge thank you to Shayak Mazumder and the Adya team for giving us access and sharing such a clear, powerful vision of the future of AI-driven development. hashtag # Adya hashtag # SmartSyllabus hashtag # AI hashtag # MultiAgentSystems hashtag # EdTech hashtag # GenerativeAI hashtag # FutureOfCoding hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence

K
KARTHIKEYAN .N
1mo
Today wasn’t just another technical session — it genuinely changed how I think about building AI products. While exploring the ADYA ecosystem and working on agentic AI systems, one idea kept coming back to me: AI isn’t about projects that run on one machine. It’s about building products that real people can actually trust and use. As part of this, I built a Terms & Conditions Risk Analyzer — an AI agent that doesn’t just summarize legal text, but helps users understand how risky an agreement really is. Building this pushed me beyond copy-paste prompts and into real problem-solving: identifying high-risk clauses like arbitration, unilateral termination, data usage, and liability limits separating clauses into positive, neutral, and concerning categories generating clear, risk-based recommendations instead of vague summaries presenting the analysis visually so users can make decisions, not guesses The biggest learning wasn’t technical — it was a mindset shift: build systems, not scripts think like a product builder, not a student launch, validate, iterate, and improve If an AI app isn’t helping users understand risk and make better choices, it’s probably just consuming compute. Now it’s time to refine, stress-test, and ship Grateful to the ADYA team for encouraging builders to move beyond demos and focus on deployable, user-centric AI. S
Shankar S
1mo
Grateful for an insightful learning experience exploring the Adya platform and its end-to-end AI ecosystem. The session provided a strong understanding of how Adya approaches AI product development through its integrated studios: 🔹 Agent Studio – Designing and orchestrating intelligent agents and workflows 🔹 App Studio – Rapidly building full-stack web applications on top of AI agents with minimal frontend or integration effort 🔹 Model Studio – Adapting and fine-tuning models for specific business use cases 🔹 Cloud Studio – Deployment, scaling, and production management As a beginner on the platform, the availability of pre-built agents and templates made it easier to understand real-world AI workflows. Using App Studio, it was impressive to see how quickly AI logic can be transformed into a functional web application. During the hands-on session, I built a simple AI agent that accepts an email as input, processes it using Gemini, and generates a concise summary along with suggested replies — a practical use case that demonstrated how ideas can move efficiently from concept to implementation. Overall, the experience reinforced the importance of an execution-driven mindset: building real systems, validating practical use cases, and delivering deployable, human-centric AI solutions rather than just theoretical prototypes. Grateful for the learning and clarity this session provided. Looking forward to building, improving, and exploring further. Monisha D

Today wasn’t just about exploring tools — it changed how I think about building with AI. Working hands-on with the Adya AI ecosystem, Agent Studio, App Studio, and Google Cloud, I realized something important: AI today isn’t about flashy demos — it’s about building deployable, usable systems. What stood out during this journey was moving beyond surface-level experimentation and into real problem-solving: Designing AI-driven workflows instead of isolated features Understanding how cloud projects, APIs, and infrastructure support scalable AI Integrating Gemini APIs to turn ideas into working applications Focusing on execution, not just concepts The biggest shift was in mindset: Build faster without getting blocked by infrastructure Think in systems, not prototypes Iterate → test → improve → deploy Aim for solutions that are production-ready, not just assignment-ready This experience reinforced one thing clearly: If we’re not building, testing, and refining real use cases, we’re just observing AI — not creating with it. Now it’s time to keep learning, keep building, and keep shipping 🚀 hashtag # Adyalaunchlab hashtag # Buildathon @adya, Shayak Mazumder ,@kamal,@balaji,@saravanamuthu hashtag # AI hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # CloudComputing hashtag # GoogleCloud hashtag # AdyaAI

M
Madhan Pungaiya
1mo
Today wasn’t just about completing a project — it was a shift in how I think about building AI-powered systems. While working on my project PROJEXA, I built it entirely on the Adya AI platform, and that experience fundamentally changed my approach to AI development. Exploring the Adya ecosystem helped me move beyond boilerplate setups and focus on real problem-solving and execution. Instead of spending time on infrastructure, I could concentrate on designing the system itself. Using Adya AI, I was able to: Design a clear agent workflow (input → reasoning → output) Define strict scope and behavior rules for the agent Rapidly iterate on prompts and logic Test real use cases in the Playground before refining the system Move from idea → structure → execution much faster Through Adya, PROJEXA evolved into an AI Project Planning & Execution Assistant, focused on clear workflows and real-world execution, not just responses. What stood out wasn’t just the speed — it was the execution mindset Adya enabled. AI stopped being about: ❌ “Which model should I use?” and became about: ✅ “What problem am I solving, and how does this system help execute it?” This experience reinforced a key learning: AI isn’t about demos or prompts — it’s about shipping usable systems. Grateful for the exposure, tooling, and clarity this journey provided. Special thanks to Adya AI Labs , Shayak Mazumder , and @Haneef Shaik for enabling builders to move from idea → execution → impact. Now it’s time to iterate, refine, and scale 🚀 hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # AI hashtag # TechWorkshop hashtag # Innovation hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # ExecutionMatters hashtag # ProductThinking hashtag # StudentBuilder hashtag # PROJEXA

Had a valuable learning experience exploring the Adya platform and gaining exposure to its end-to-end AI ecosystem. The session provided a clear understanding of how Adya approaches AI product development through its integrated studios: Agent Studio – for designing and orchestrating intelligent agents and workflows App Studio – for rapidly building complete web applications on top of AI agents without heavy frontend or integration overhead Model Studio – for adapting and fine-tuning models for specific business use cases Cloud Studio – for deployment, scaling, and production management As a beginner exploring the platform, I found the availability of pre-built agents and templates extremely helpful in understanding real-world AI workflows. Using App Studio, it was insightful to see how quickly AI logic can be transformed into a functional web application. During the hands-on session, I built a simple AI agent that takes an email as input, processes it using Gemini, and generates a concise summary along with suggested replies — a practical use case that highlighted the platform’s ability to move ideas swiftly from concept to implementation. Overall, the experience reinforced the importance of an execution-focused approach: building real systems, testing practical use cases, and delivering deployable, human-centric AI solutions rather than just prototypes. Grateful for the learning and clarity this session provided. Looking forward to iterating, improving, and building further. Had a great learning experience exploring the Adya platform and its end-to-end AI ecosystem 🚀. The session gave a clear view of how Adya structures AI product development through its different studios: Agent Studio 🤖 for designing and orchestrating AI agents and workflows. App Studio 🧩 for building complete web applications on top of these agents, without getting stuck in frontend or integration complexity. Model Studio 🧠 for fine-tuning and adapting models to specific use cases. Cloud Studio ☁️ for deployment, scaling, and managing applications in production. In the hands-on session, I explored the platform as a beginner and found the availability of pre-built agents and templates extremely helpful. Using App Studio, it became clear how quickly AI logic can be converted into a usable web application. I built a simple agent that takes an email as input, processes it through Gemini, and returns a concise summary along with suggested replies ✉️—a small but practical use case that showed how fast ideas can move from concept to working application. Overall, the experience emphasized a strong execution mindset build real systems, test real use cases, and focus on deployable, human-centric AI rather than just demos. Grateful for the exposure and clarity this session provided. Time to iterate, refine, and ship . KARTHIKA K
1mo
Built an AI-Powered Agriculture Management System Recently worked on developing a full-stack AI-powered agriculture management application focused on practical, real-world use cases. The system helps farmers make data-driven decisions by analyzing inputs such as soil type, location, season, crop history, and market trends. The application provides intelligent crop recommendations, yield predictions, irrigation and fertilizer guidance, and market insights to support better planning and profitability. AI is integrated to deliver explainable recommendations while keeping usability simple and farmer-friendly. This project strengthened my understanding of full-stack development, AI integration, and building scalable solutions with real social impact. It also highlighted the importance of designing responsible and accessible AI systems for real users. India Digital Summit
1mo
Adya — An AI orchestration platform that helps businesses build, deploy, and scale intelligent AI apps and workflows with enterprise-grade automation. SuperBryn — SuperBryn is a Voice AI reliability platform built for teams deploying voice agents in production. It helps monitor, evaluate, and understand real voice interactions at scale. SuperBryn shows where voice agents fail, why issues happen, and how to improve performance. Teams use it to ensure voice AI is stable, measurable, and production-ready. M
Muhammad Hatta I.
1mo
Over the past year, I had the opportunity to contribute to UNESCO ’s AI Readiness Assessment Methodology (AI RAM) in Lao PDR, which is being released today at a national workshop in Vientiane. This was new territory for me: navigating a different institutional and cultural context, understanding Laos’ digital ecosystem before arriving, and translating global AI framework (adoption readiness especially) into national context. A huge shoutout to my buddy Adya Danaditya , the AI expert without borders who tagged me along on this journey as we learned and contributed together while listening deeply to policymakers, educators, civil society, private companies, and communities. Some key learnings that stuck with me throughout this whole experience: - AI is still in its nascent stage in Laos, but conversations with stakeholders were clear about what matters first: putting safeguards in place, building trust between those who design systems and those who use them, and investing steadily in people and not just tools - Lao language have unique challenges in terms of NLP and LLMs due to limited resources, thus bringing awareness to national AI sovereignty and culture preservation (which is a concern for many in Global South I think) - I am really glad that the readiness assessment became a catalyst for discussion, and not being treated as a verdict. Instead of scoring capability, it opened grounded discussions about what could realistically come next We are excited to see how Laos will continue shaping its AI governance journey in the coming years, and very keen to learn from others working on AI adoption, implementation, and its impact on societies and organizations in different contexts. If you’re involved in AI policy, public sector transformation, tech adoption, or digital ethics (especially in the Global South), would love to connect and learn more! Links: - UNESCO country profile and key insights for Laos: https://lnkd.in/gJ3dDF25 - Full AI RAM report: https://lnkd.in/g3K4a3rv

Had an insightful experience participating in the Adya Launch Lab Buildathon conducted by the Department of AI & DS in association with IEEE, IEDC and TinkerHub. The session gave a strong introduction to the vision behind Adya and how AI agents can be used to build real, usable products. D
Diya Joshy
2mo
The Adya AI Launch Lab was truly an eye-opening experience. The session clearly underscored both the urgency and the opportunity for India to evolve from being a consumer of AI to becoming a creator of AI. The energy and clarity with which the Adya team drove this message made the experience both inspiring and thought-provoking. I felt especially privileged to participate in the mini buildathon, where our team set out to design a governed AI tutoring system powered by a four-agent architecture that dynamically adapts teaching strategies based on learner behavior. The system brings together a Learner Profiler, Adaptive Tutor, Teacher Insight Agent, and an Adaptation Auditor, working collectively to ensure personalization, transparency, and responsible AI-driven learning. As a strong first step, we successfully built a functional dashboard supporting both tutor and student profiles. This foundation opens up exciting possibilities, and I look forward to exploring the platform further and iterating on the idea. Grateful to Shayak Mazumder and the entire Adya team for organizing such a power-driven and purpose-led event. hashtag # Adya hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AIOnCampus hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # StudentInnovation hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # BuildInPublic

A
Albert M Paul
2mo
🚜🔍 AI Tractor Invoice Processing Agent — built on Adya AI Our team — Basil Saji, Alwin Sony, Abhinavkrishna B, and myself — built an end-to-end agent during the Adya buildathon to automate tractor dealer quotation processing for loan workflows. Using Adya Flow Builder + multimodal LLMs, the agent: • extracts structured data from handwritten/printed invoices • returns strict JSON (model, HP, price) • runs validation checks against a master list • flags anomalies automatically for review The result: faster underwriting, fewer manual errors, and a workflow-first approach (ingestion → intelligence → validation → action). Appreciate the insightful session from @Shayak Mazumdar on designing AI systems that solve real business problems. hashtag # Adya hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AIOnCampus hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # StudentInnovation hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # FutureBuilders hashtag # TechInnovation hashtag # GenAI hashtag # Fintech hashtag # Hackathon hashtag # ProcessAutomation hashtag # MachineLearning

M
Malavika Rajan
2mo
🚀 𝗡𝗲𝘄 𝘆𝗲𝗮𝗿 𝗞𝗶𝗰𝗸𝗼𝗳𝗳 — Curiosity, Chaos, and an AI FinOps Agent Built at the Last Minute 👀 Started the year in the most unexpected (but exciting) way — a one-day workshop & 𝘣𝘶𝘪𝘭𝘥𝘢𝘵𝘩𝘰𝘯 with 𝗔𝗱𝘆𝗮, in collaboration with 𝗔𝗦𝗜𝗘𝗧 𝗔𝗜 &𝗗𝗦 𝗱𝗲𝗽𝘁, 𝗜𝗘𝗘𝗘, 𝗜𝗘𝗗𝗖 and 𝗧𝗶𝗻𝗸𝗲𝗿𝗵𝘂𝗯 And yes… I wrapped up the day by shipping a working prototype of a Cloud & IT Cost Overruns Agent 😄 ________________________________________ 🔍 𝕄𝕠𝕣𝕟𝕚𝕟𝕘 — The “We-Don’t-Know-This-Yet” Exploration Sprint The first ~45 minutes were basically a fast-paced exploration run through: “𝙾𝚔𝚊𝚢… 𝚕𝚎𝚝'𝚜 𝚎𝚡𝚙𝚕𝚘𝚛𝚎 𝙴𝚅𝙴𝚁𝚈𝚃𝙷𝙸𝙽𝙶 𝙰𝙸... 𝚏𝚊𝚜𝚝” 😅 We skimmed across: • latent space & generative AI • agents & multi-agent workflows • LLMs, SLMs & foundational models • open-model ecosystems — Llama, Qwen, Mistral, Gemma • deployment stacks like vLLM & Hugging Face runtimes Not a deep lecture — more like: speed-running the AI universe just to catch up 🧭 And honestly, that made it fun. 🏦 𝗕𝗼𝗻𝘂𝘀 𝘀𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻— HDFC Bank x Startup Insights The day wrapped up with HDFC Bank sharing: • startup support initiatives • tech partnerships • benefits & growth programs Nice bridge between building → real-world ecosystem. ________________________________________ 🧩 𝗔𝗳𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗼𝗼𝗻 — Enter Adya Studios (aka The Distraction Zone) Then we moved into: • Agent Studio • App Studio • Cloud Studio And that’s where the real chaos began. We started exploring… clicked one feature… then another… then another… and suddenly — “𝚆𝚊𝚒𝚝... 𝚊𝚛𝚎𝚗'𝚝 𝚠𝚎 𝚜𝚞𝚙𝚙𝚘𝚜𝚎𝚡 𝚝𝚘 𝚋𝚎 𝚋𝚞𝚒𝚕𝚍𝚒𝚗𝚐 𝚜𝚘𝚖𝚎𝚝𝚑𝚒𝚗𝚐?” 😭 We almost spent the whole time just experimenting — but somehow still pulled off a working prototype in the final stretch 😄 𝐂𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐨𝐬𝐢𝐭𝐲 + 𝐝𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐞 = 𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐥𝐲 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐛𝐨.. ________________________________________ 💡 𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗜 𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱 — Cloud & IT Cost Overruns Agent The prototype helps: • track cloud spend across AWS / Azure / GCP • detect cost spikes & under-utilized resources • provide weekly budget insights • recommend optimization (rightsizing / reservations) • share insights with a forecasting agent Not over-engineered — just meaningful, FinOps-focused, and practical. Small build — big direction forward. ________________________________________ 🙌 Grateful for the Experience Huge thanks to: • Adya AI Labs team especially Shayak Mazumder Sir • Adi Shankara Institute of Engineering and Technology, Kalady AI & DS Dept • IEEE | IEDC Kerala | TinkerHub for creating a space where learning, experimentation, and building all came together in one day. Amazing start to the year — and I’m definitely continuing this build 🚀 hashtag # Adya hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AIOnCampus hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # StudentInnovation hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # FutureBuilders hashtag # TechInnovation

Had an amazing time attending a session on Adya.ai and participating in their Buildathon 🚀 Adya.ai is India’s own AI automation and agent-creation platform, and the session was a fantastic deep dive into how AI agents can be built, deployed, and scaled effectively. It was an incredible learning opportunity to explore real-world AI applications and build hands-on solutions. During the buildathon, I worked on creating an AI agent that: Takes a resume as input and uses an LLM to ensure its content is well-aligned with a given job description Includes an additional feature (currently in development) that leverages GitHub MCP to analyze repositories mentioned in the resume and score projects based on their relevance and quality Big thanks to Shayak Mazumder Sir for the guidance and insights, and kudos to Adya AI Labs for creating such a powerful platform and enabling student innovation on campus. Looking forward to building more and refining this agent further! 🙌 hashtag # Adya hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AIOnCampus hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # StudentInnovation hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # FutureBuilders hashtag # TechInnovation

🧘🏻‍♂🍃 I got hands-on with AI at the Adya Buildathon 🧘🏻‍♂🍃 Spent an intense and exciting day at the Adya Buildathon, where the goal wasn’t to just talk about AI but to actually build with it. During the event, I worked on creating a UI powered by Adya AI Agents, exploring how intelligent agents can directly shape user experience. Instead of treating AI as something happening behind the scenes, the focus was on making it visible, interactive, and meaningful through design. What I appreciated most was the environment: fast paced, collaborative, and builder-centric. You’re encouraged to experiment, break things, fix them, and move forward without waiting for “perfect.” That mindset alone made the experience incredibly valuable. This buildathon reinforced an important lesson for me: The future isn’t just about knowing AI it’s about knowing how to work alongside it. When human creativity and AI capabilities align, building becomes faster, smarter, and more impactful. I’m walking away with: • A functional AI driven UI • Practical exposure to AI agents • Stronger confidence in rapid prototyping • And a clearer direction toward AI powered product development Big thanks to the Adya.ai team and mentors for enabling students to turn ideas into real, working solutions. This is just the beginning. project : https://lnkd.in/diKSdhAW 🧘🏻‍♂🍃 hashtag # AdyaBuildathon hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # UIUX hashtag # ProductBuilding hashtag # HandsOnLearning hashtag # AIBuilders hashtag # TechJourney

An AI buildathon! I participated in Adya Launch Lab : AI on Campus, where I developed an Operational Reliability & Downtime Prevention AI Agent. The solution analyzes system health metrics to identify early indicators of failure, assesses downtime risk, and delivers explainable alerts with preventive recommendations using a human-in-the-loop framework. This experience provided valuable hands-on exposure to building AI agents for real-world reliability and proactive decision-making. A
Apoorva Raman
2mo
✨ Building My First AI Agent of 2026 ✨ I kicked off 2026 by attending the Adya Launch Lab Buildathon on 5 January, and walked away with something far more valuable than just notes or takeaways: a working AI prototype. At this one-day workshop, conducted in collaboration with Adya AI, the Department of AI & DS (ASIET), IEEE, IEDC, and TinkerHub, I built a Career Path Discovery AI agent using the Adya AI platform. The agent interacts with users to understand their interests, skills, personality traits, and work preferences, and then generates: • a personalized profile summary • six relevant career options with match percentages • insights like salary range, demand, remote feasibility, and satisfaction • and a detailed action plan for careers they want to explore further What made this experience stand out was not just building, but the perspective shared by Shayak Sir. One idea that really stayed with me was his emphasis on how AI, while it may not “feel,” can reflect empathy consistently - without fatigue, bias, or judgment. In many cases, it already outperforms humans in accuracy, especially in critical fields like healthcare. Another powerful takeaway was about mindset. Despite having immense talent, we often hesitate to build. We look for roles instead of creating solutions. This session existed for one reason: to change that. I walked away with clarity, confidence, and a functioning product. AI alone can do around 55% of the work. Humans alone bring about 45%. But when humans and AI work together, that potential rises to 65% and beyond. Learning how to work with AI is what will truly shape the future. Here’s the prototype I built - feel free to try it and let me know how close it hits to your own interests and career direction: https://lnkd.in/gxVZqZHN Grateful to Shayak Mazumder and the Adya team for pushing students to build, not just learn. hashtag # Adya hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AIOnCampus hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # StudentInnovation hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # FutureBuilders hashtag # TechInnovation

🌟 Thrilled to share my experience at the Adya Launch Lab – AI on Campus event! 🌟 Being part of this initiative was truly inspiring. The sessions gave me deep insights into AI Agents, innovation, entrepreneurship, and building in public, while also showing how students like us can turn ideas into real-world impact using Artificial Intelligence. What I loved most was the hands-on learning environment and collaborative spirit — discussing ideas, solving problems together, and interacting with mentors really helped me understand how powerful AI can be when applied with curiosity and purpose. 🚀 Super grateful to Shayak Sir and the Adya team for creating such a meaningful platform for students to learn, experiment, and grow. Opportunities like this push us to think beyond the classroom and step confidently into the future of tech. 💡✨ I’m excited to keep exploring, building, and sharing my journey ahead — because this is just the beginning! hashtag # Adya hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # AIOnCampus hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # StudentInnovation hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # BuildInPublic hashtag # FutureBuilders hashtag # TechInnovation Shayak Mazumder Adya

Attended the Adya Launch Lab – AI on Campus session recently, and it genuinely changed how I look at learning AI 👀 Instead of just theory or slides, we actually built a simple AI agent hands-on, which made the concepts feel clear and practical. It was a strong reminder of how quickly the tech and job landscape is changing, and why building things matters more than passively consuming content. The session also motivated me to apply these ideas to my own projects. I’m currently developing a personal finance tracker website that helps users monitor spending, analyze expenses, and build healthier financial habits. Thinking about how AI can add value to something so practical made the whole learning process feel exciting and real. Bharath Krishnan S

🚀 AI Recruiter – AI-Powered Recruitment Management Platform (Built with Adya AI) AI Recruiter is an AI-driven recruitment management platform built using Adya AI, designed to streamline the entire hiring lifecycle through intelligent automation, bias-aware decision-making, and real-time recruitment analytics. The platform leverages AI, NLP, and candidate scoring models to: ✔️ Automatically parse and screen resumes ✔️ Rank candidates based on role-fit and profile matching ✔️ Detect potential bias patterns and generate fairness alerts ✔️ Provide real-time recruitment insights through a unified dashboard ✔️ Maintain audit logs for transparency and compliance ✔️ Manage and track multiple job postings efficiently 📊 Recruitment Dashboard Highlights Active candidates in the hiring pipeline Total resumes processed by AI Bias alerts supporting fair hiring practices Average AI-based screening time 🔄 Candidate Pipeline Applied → Screened → Shortlisted → Interviewed → Hired Each stage provides conversion metrics to enable data-driven hiring decisions. 🎯 Target Users HR Recruiters | Talent Acquisition Teams | Hiring Managers | Organizations seeking scalable and fair hiring solutions 📈 Key Benefits Faster hiring • Reduced manual screening • Bias-aware recruitment • Actionable insights • Improved candidate experience 🙏 Special thanks to Shayak Mazumder and Adya team for the guidance and leadership behind Adya AI, which made building and exploring this solution possible. hashtag # AdyaAI hashtag # ArtificialIntelligence hashtag # HRTech hashtag # AIRecruitment hashtag # MachineLearning hashtag # HiringAutomation hashtag # EnterpriseAI hashtag # ProductEngineering

Exploring AI & Startup Building at Adya Launch Lab | CIT Chennai I had the opportunity to attend the Adya Launch Lab Roadshow held on 15th & 16th December 2025 at Chennai Institute of Technology (CIT Chennai). The sessions offered valuable insights into AI-driven startup building, product development, agent-based systems, and how students can effectively transform ideas into real-world solutions. The conversations around innovation, execution, and the evolving startup ecosystem were both insightful and motivating. Grateful to Shayak Mazumder , Founder of Adya AI & Adya Launch Lab, for sharing his vision and practical perspectives on building scalable AI products. Built a Supply Chain Visibility & Disruption Management Agent with ADYA AI Recently, I got the opportunity to attend the Adya AI Labs event at my college. The sessions gave me strong insights into vibe coding, LLMs, how products move from the idea stage to real-world use, and how agent-based systems actually work. The focus on execution over theory made it really interesting. During the program, I built a Supply Chain Visibility & Disruption Management Agent, which helped me understand how AI agents can monitor workflows, identify disruptions, and support decision-making in real scenarios. Thanks to Shayak Mazumder , Founder of Adya AI & Adya Launch Lab, for sharing realistic insights and scalable AI solutions. Overall, it was a useful and motivating experience. Learned a lot and got more clarity on building solutions that actually work ✨ hashtag # AdyaLaunchLab hashtag # CITChennai hashtag # AIAgents hashtag # StudentBuilders hashtag # SupplyChain hashtag # AIDevelopment
